title: osmunda regalis subsp. japonica
intro: Osmunda regalis subsp. japonica, also known as Japanese royal fern, is a subspecies of the Osmunda regalis plant, a type of fern native to Japan and parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of various compounds such as flavonoids and triterpenes. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and supporting immune function, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the Japanese royal fern is often prepared as a tea or decoction, and it can also be used in modern herbal preparations, such as capsules and tinctures, although its use should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
